data.title: Meyer Burger skyrockets after securing $39.5M bridge loan following key client's exit
data.description:
Meyer Burger Technology (OTCPK:MYBUF) +219% in European trading on Friday after the Swiss solar panel maker said it secured $39.48M in bridge financing, including an immediate $19.7M drawdown, to stabilize its embattled financial position.

The secured loan comes after the company's top client, D.E. Shaw Renewable Investments, terminated its master agreement with the company last month.

Meyer Burger (OTCPK:MYBUF) said it is in advanced talks with D.E. Shaw Renewable about a new offtake deal with updated terms, which it hopes to conclude later this month.

Meyer Burger (OTCPK:MYBUF) Executive Chairman Franz Richter said he saw interest from D.E. Shaw Renewable and IKEA operator Ingka in solar cells not made in China, especially after the election of Donald Trump as U.S. president, who is expected to raise tariffs on Chinese imports; Richter said renewed interest in the U.S. partly helped secure the bridge loan.

Richter said the bridge loan should fund sufficient liquidity for Meyer Burger (OTCPK:MYBUF) to reach agreements with bondholders and DESRI to reach a sustainable restructuring solution to stabilize its long-term finances.

data.title: Wells Fargo’s ‘SMID-cap’ List: TKO, BRBR, CPAY, and more!
data.description:
Wells Fargo Investment Institute highlighted their best picks for small-caps (SP600) and mid-caps (SP400).

These are companies with a market cap ranging from $1-20B and the objective is to exceed the total return of the S&P 1000 (both mid- and small-cap benchmarks combined) over the long term.

From Jan. 1, 1994, through Dec. 30, 2023, small- and mid-cap stocks combined have tended to outperform large-cap stocks (SP500).

“We believe shareholders with a long-term perspective and a willingness to endure the periodic volatility that comes with this asset class have the potential to enjoy attractive returns from small- and mid-cap companies as they grow and mature,” said Robert Hammel, equity sector analyst at Wells Fargo, in a note.

To come up with the list of favorite stocks, Wells Fargo analysts look for companies with “understandable accounting and acceptable governance standards,” as well as companies that are well-capitalized and “can meet their principal, interest, and dividend obligations under a variety of macroeconomic scenarios.”

Here is the list:

Within communication services

  • TKO Group Holdings (TKO)

Within consumer discretionary

  • Burlington Stores (BURL)
  • Domino’s Pizza (DPZ)
  • Vail Resorts (MTN)
  • Ulta Beauty (ULTA)
  • BellRing Brands (BRBR)
  • McCormick & Co. (MKC)
  • Post Holdings (POST)

Within financials

  • Brown & Brown (BRO)
  • Corpay (CPAY)
  • FactSet Research Systems (FDS)
  • RLI Corp. (RLI)
  • W.R. Berkley (WRB)

Within health care

  • Chemed (CHE)
  • West Pharmaceutical Services (WST)

Within industrials

  • GXO Logistics (GXO)
  • Hubbell (HUBB)
  • Lincoln Electric Holdings (LECO)
  • RBC Bearings (RBC)
  • Rollins (ROL)
  • Waste Connections (WCN)

Within information technology

  • Guidewire Software (GWRE)
  • Keysight Technologies (KEYS)
  • Tyler Technologies (TYL)

Within materials

  • RPM International (RPM)

Within real estate

  • Agree Realty (ADC)
  • FirstService (FSV)
  • STAG Industrial (STAG)
  • Sun Communities (SUI)

Within utilities

  • Brookfield Renewable (BEPC)

data.title: Edgewater announces $1.1M private placement; shares up
data.description:
• Edgewater Wireless Systems (TSXV:YFI:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 22M units at a price of $0.05 per unit for gross proceeds of up to $1.1M.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one common share purchase warrant, each warrant entitling the holder to purchase one additional common share of the company at an exercise price of $0.08 per share for a period of two years from the closing date.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds for product commercialization and general working capital.
  • YFI:CA shares were up 12.5% on Friday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Brixmor Property upgraded at BMO on 'continued solid retail sales and leasing'
data.description:
Shopping center REIT Brixmor Property Group (NYSE:BRX) was upgraded at BMO Capital Markets on "continued solid retail sales and leasing".

"BRX has meaningfully improved its portfolio over the past several years, with attractive, low basis rents, amble redevelopment opportunities, a strong balance sheet, and well-respected/deep management with increased accretive acquisitions opportunities now in play," said analyst Juan Sanabria in a research note.

Given attractive relative growth, we expect continued multiple expansion vs. peers with tenant credit conditions benign," added Sanabria.

The note said Brixmor's relative FFO multiple has expanded but remains attractive vs. peers; the $507M redevelopments pipeline drives future growth, occupancy and releasing upside, and NAV value creation; a strong leasing momentum gives BMO visibility/confidence on earnings growth; the company has captured leading net effective rent spreads with in-line capex; and leverage is healthy at 5.7x net debt/EBITDA.

BMO Capital Markets raised its recommendation on the stock to Outperform from Market perform, and revised the price target to $33 from $30.

The rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system grades BRX as Hold.

"BRX's exposure to low tenant quality, non-essential retail and below average household income, is a clear disadvantage," said SA contributor Elias Mouawad.

"My dividend discount model adds to the conviction that BRX does not deserve any additional upside, with the current stock slightly overvalued. Thus, a "sector perform" rating is appropriate for the stock," said the author.

data.title: CERo Therapeutics appoints Chris Ehrlich CEO
data.description:
• CERo Therapeutics Holdings (NASDAQ:CERO) has appointed Chris Ehrlich as CEO. 
  • Previously Ehrlich held the position of Interim CEO.
  • In addition to his role at CERo, Ehrlich currently serves as CEO of Launch One Acquisition Corporation.
  • CERO shares were down 29.5% on Friday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Profound stock slides 6% on $35M public offering pricing
data.description:
Profound Medical (NASDAQ:PROF) stock slid 6% in early trading Friday after it announced the pricing of a $35M public offering of its shares.

The Canadian medical device maker priced 4.67M shares at $7.50 per share, which will raise gross proceeds of around $35M. Underwriters have been granted a 30-day option to buy up to 700K additional shares.

Net proceeds from the offering will go towards funding commercialization of the company’s TULSA-PRO system in the United States, development and commercialization of the TULSA-PRO and Sonalleve systems globally, and general corporate purposes, according to a statement.

data.title: Consumer sentiment drifts up in December, even with higher year-ahead inflation expectations
data.description:
• University of Michigan's U.S. Consumer Sentiment: 74.0 vs. 73.0 consensus and 71.8 in November.
  • Consumer Expectations: 71.6 vs. 76.9 prior.
  • Current Conditions: 77.7 vs. 63.9 prior.
  • Year-ahead inflation expectations: 2.9% vs. 2.6% prior.
  • Five-year implied inflation: 3.1% vs. 3.2% in November.

data.title: PJT Partners upgraded to Neutral at UBS as capital markets reopen
data.description:
UBS raised its recommendation on PJT Partners (NYSE:PJT) to Neutral from Sell as the firm's investments in its strategic advisory business and the sustainability of its restructuring practice should deliver "substantial operating leverage as capital markets reopen."

PJT stock gained 2.1% in Friday morning trading. At Thursday's close, the stock climbed 16% in the past month, outpacing the S&P 500's 5.4% increase.

The stock's recent re-rating is concerning, said analyst Brennan Hawken, but by his estimates, consensus forecasts look too low, "which makes it hard to argue for underperformance."

Also in PJT Partners' (NYSE:PJT) favor, it has a stronger public deal pipeline, rising 12% Q/Q, than its peers, which declined 19%.

"With a focus on large-cap deals (>$1B) and 88% of its revenue base derived domestically, PJT is well positioned to capture greater wallet share as capital markets continue to recover," Hawken wrote in a note to clients.

UBS's Neutral rating on PJT (PJT) aligns with the SA Quant rating and the average Wall Street rating, both at Hold.

data.title: Peloton's anemic growth outlook expected to produce more cost-cutting - analyst
data.description:
Peloton’s (NASDAQ:PTON) outlook remains uncertain and the company’s success in luring in new subscribers is “limited.” But UBS analysts Arpine Kocharyan and Robin Farley upgraded the stock to Neutral from Sell, as the expectation for further EBITDA gains from cost-cutting and aligning the CEO’s pay to the company’s performance encourages a more objective outlook for the company.

“We believe more muted subscriber growth could necessitate further cost optimization, primarily within opex,” Kocharyan and Farley write in Friday’s research note, which in turn will support EBITDA, revised upward to $298M from $216M in FY25, and to $376M from $277M in FY27. The majority of the cuts will come from sales and marketing ($50M), followed by contractor spending and supply chain costs ($15M) and $10M from IT and software, this on top of the $100M from a 15% headcount reduction, and another $100M in other costs previously announced.

Peloton’s (PTON) growth outlook remains uncertain, UBS says, and higher subscription pricing the company is expected to implement will further pressure subscriber growth. This could lead to little or no growth for the company over the next 2 years, evidenced by the 5% decline in unique visitors to the Peloton (PTON) site in October after strong growth in September. And UBS’s data shows monthly active users dropped 19% in October year-over-year from an 11% decline the month prior.

Along with the upgrade, UBS analysts also raised their target price for Peloton (PTON) to $10 to reflect the adjusted EV/sales multiple and recent gains in the stock.

The upgrade is propping up the stock in early trading, with PTON gaining more than 8% at the open.

data.title: Arcadium Lithium gains amid China filing on Rio Tinto deal
data.description:
Arcadium Lithium (NYSE:ALTM) rose 1.7% after a filing in China related to the lithium company's planned $6.7 billion to Rio Tinto (RIO).

The transaction was filed under "simplified procedure" to a regional office of the company's main antitrust authority, according to a posting on Chongqing Administration for Market Regulation's website that was circulating among traders on Friday.

The Arcadium (ALTM) deal has raised some potential national security worries in the U.S. as Aluminum Corp of China (OTCPK:ALMMF) holds a 14.99% stake in Rio Tinto (RIO). Lithium is a key ingredient in rechargeable batteries, including those for electric vehicles.
